vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1824: Re-land the @slow exclusion for the conversation-supervisor Playwright UAT (root cause already fixed by fd7b0cbe1; fake-timer conversion forbidden)

plan.title: Re-land @slow exclusion for the conversation-supervisor Playwright UAT
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The conversation-supervisor Playwright UAT (tests/playwright/conversation-supervisor-uat.test.ts) is a wall-clock real-browser (chromium.launch) integration test that runs in the default `vitest run` / CI `npm test`. It was one of the ~5s-timeout victims on loaded CI runners. The actual root cause (PAN-1798's managed tmux-server founding burning 5s synchronously inside every createSession under tests) was already fixed on main by fd7b0cbe1, and the whole family now passes fast (verified in CI run 27472211377: the UAT itself ✓2936ms). An earlier strike (d9807686d) ALSO tagged this UAT @slow to keep wall-clock browser tests out of the gating default run — but that commit bundled fake-timer conversions of the tmux/IO suites which hung both main CI jobs for the full 30-min budget, so the whole commit was reverted (53ac34014). The revert took the harmless, correct @slow tagging down with the harmful conversion, so the wall-clock UAT is back in the default suite where a loaded-runner timeout on it would redden main and mask real regressions.
narratives.Proposal: Re-land ONLY the @slow exclusion, replaying exactly the two known-good hunks of d9807686d. (1) Declare a @slow tag in vitest.config.ts test config and set tagsFilter to ['!@slow'] so the default run excludes @slow-tagged tests. (2) Tag the single UAT describe with { tags: ['@slow'] } and prove the default `vitest run` collects zero tests from that file. (3) Add a `test:slow` npm script (`vitest run --tagsFilter @slow`) so the excluded set stays runnable on demand. Do NOT redo any fake-timer conversion — the root cause is already fixed and that approach hangs CI. Because @slow exclusion is pure deterministic config-level tag filtering (identical local and CI, independent of whether tmux/IO ops succeed), local verification that the default run skips the UAT is a faithful CI proxy.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Do NOT convert codex.test.ts, conversations.test.ts, reviewer-tree.test.ts, or any fork/heartbeat/kill/conversation-routes test to fake timers — that approach (d9807686d) hung CI for 30 min and was reverted (53ac34014).
- Do NOT modify src/lib/tmux.ts or the fd7b0cbe1 founding guard — the root cause is already fixed there.
- Do NOT modify .github/workflows/ci.yml (e.g. removing the now-unused root chromium install, or adding a dedicated @slow CI job) — follow-up only.
- Do NOT introduce any vitest tag other than @slow; strictTags defaults to true and an undeclared tag errors the whole suite.
- Do NOT add new wall-clock/real-timer tests; any genuinely wall-clock test must be @slow-tagged.
- Do NOT add a config-assertion regression-guard test for the @slow filter — possible follow-up, out of scope here.

autoDecisions.summary: Do NOT redo any fake-timer conversion.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue body asks for it, but the operator's later comments authoritatively re-scope: the root cause is already fixed by fd7b0cbe1 and the conversion hung both main CI jobs and was reverted. Later operator comments supersede the stale body; chronological re-scope, not an unresolvable contradiction.

autoDecisions.summary: Re-land @slow by replaying exactly the two known-good hunks of d9807686d, nothing else.
autoDecisions.rationale: The @slow mechanism was API-correct vitest 4.x; only the bundled conversion was harmful. Verbatim replay minimizes risk.

autoDecisions.summary: Add a `test:slow` npm script so the excluded @slow set stays runnable on demand.
autoDecisions.rationale: An excluded-from-default set with no runner is write-only / silent coverage loss; the project rule is 'no silent caps'. Kept as its own droppable bead.

autoDecisions.summary: Do NOT touch the CI workflow or add a config-guard test.
autoDecisions.rationale: Out of the operator's explicit 'ONLY' scope; recorded as NonGoal follow-ups. Karpathy simplicity-first.

items.title: Declare the @slow tag and exclude it from the default vitest run
Given vitest.config.ts, the `test` config declares a @slow tag via `tags: [{ name: '@slow', description: 'Wall-clock integration tests' }]`
Given `tagsFilter: ['!@slow']` in the `test` config, a default `vitest run` excludes (does not execute) @slow-tagged tests
When no test is yet tagged, `npx vitest run` still passes the existing suite — the declared-but-unused @slow tag is accepted under strictTags (does not error)
Tag the conversation-supervisor Playwright UAT @slow and prove the default run excludes it
The single `describe('conversation supervisor Playwright UAT', ...)` is given `{ tags: ['@slow'] }` as its second argument
When `npx vitest run tests/playwright/conversation-supervisor-uat.test.ts` runs with the default config, it executes zero tests (the file's test is filtered out by tagsFilter `!@slow`)
`npm run typecheck` exits 0 and passes — the 3-arg `describe(name, { tags }, fn)` signature compiles under vitest 4.1.6

When a default `npm test` runs, it does not execute the UAT test 'delivers through real conversation routes and keeps plain forks off Channels MCP' — the test name is absent from the default-run output
Add a test:slow npm script so the excluded @slow set stays runnable on demand

narrative.Action: Add a `test:slow` script to package.json `scripts` defined as `vitest run --tagsFilter @slow` (the inverse of the default `!@slow` exclusion; --tagsFilter is the verified vitest 4.1.6 CLI flag). This keeps the @slow set runnable on demand rather than silently orphaned. Note the @slow UAT uses real chromium.launch(), so a green run requires chromium installed (`npx playwright install chromium`); the bead's AC only requires that the script SELECTS the @slow test for execution, not that the browser test passes in every environment. Place the script next to the existing `test` / `test:unit` entries.

items.title: When `npm run test:slow` runs, vitest selects the @slow-tagged UAT test for execution rather than reporting zero matched tests (the inverse of the default exclusion)
`npm run lint` passes with the new script present (no JSON or skills-lint drift)
